extracted.brandBackground: Certified disabled Veteran-owned mobile fuel delivery company founded in 2023, specializing in on-site delivery of diesel, gasoline, and DEF to fleets, equipment, and generators across commercial and industrial markets.
extracted.conceptRationale: Mobile on-demand fuel delivery to commercial fleets and job sites is a B2B trade/field-service business with no retail storefront.
extracted.staffingRationale: Item 15 allows absentee ownership with a designated manager; Item 11 describes a small-crew driver/dispatch model with one to a few vehicles per territory.

leadership.name: Cory Albertson
leadership.background: Co-founded WetFuel, LLC in 2023 and WetFuel Franchising, LLC in July 2025; previously co-owned Craftsman Unlimited Haircuts (2017–2021).
leadership.whyItMatters: Primary decision-maker and face of the franchise system with direct operating experience in the affiliate unit.

leadership.name: Stephen Thomas
leadership.background: Co-founded WetFuel, LLC in 2023; prior fuel and oil delivery experience at Co-Energy Fuel Delivery (2014–2023).
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings direct wet-hose fueling industry expertise and leads franchisee training programs.

item19.notes: Single affiliate-owned outlet (WetFuel, LLC) in Boise, ID MSA (~845,000 people), period April 20, 2025 – April 10, 2026. Franchisor warns results may not be representative of franchisee results and that smaller markets may produce lower sales. No franchised outlet data disclosed. Adjusted Net Income deducts estimated royalty, brand fund, local advertising, and tech fees that the affiliate did not actually pay.

cohorts.label: Affiliate-Owned Outlet – Gross Sales
cohorts.basis: Total income for the period April 20, 2025 – April 10, 2026 from single affiliate outlet in Boise, ID.

cohorts.label: Affiliate-Owned Outlet – Gross Profit
cohorts.basis: Total revenue minus cost of goods sold (fuel, DEF, oils) for the same period.

cohorts.label: Affiliate-Owned Outlet – Net Income
cohorts.basis: Gross Profit minus total operating expenses plus other income minus other expenses for the period.

cohorts.label: Affiliate-Owned Outlet – Adjusted Net Income (after estimated franchise fees)
cohorts.basis: Net Income minus estimated royalty ($97,792.68), brand fund ($21,731.71), local advertising ($21,731.71), and tech fees ($6,000); does not include owner compensation.

lineItems.notes: Paid to franchisor upon signing; non-refundable except partial refund if training not completed.
One month rent plus security deposit estimate; Item 7, p.17.
Build-out/remodel of office or storage location; Item 7, p.17.

lineItems.category: Market Introduction Program and Opening Advertising
lineItems.notes: Includes opening advertising minimum $750/month for first 6 months; Item 7, p.17.
Furniture, fixtures, design elements, and equipment; Item 7, p.17.
Hardware and software; $1,500–$4,000 of this paid to franchisor for tech setup; Item 7, p.17.
Commercial general liability, auto, pollution, umbrella, workers comp; Item 7, p.17.
$30K = tank/pump in own truck bed; $60K = fuel trailer; $170K = F550 fully outfitted; Item 7, p.17.
Exterior and interior signage and vehicle wrap; Item 7, p.17.
Fuel inventory plus two fuel storage tanks ($16,000–$60,000 of total); Item 7, p.18.
Motor fuel transporter, mobile fueling, and other regulatory permits; Item 7, p.18.
Attorney, CPA, franchise attorney review; Item 7, p.18.

lineItems.category: Travel, Lodging and Meals for Initial Training
lineItems.notes: Franchisee responsible for travel/lodging to Boise, ID training; Item 7, p.18.
Working capital for payroll, inventory, rent, and operating expenses; does not include owner salary; Item 7, p.18.

hiddenCosts.name: Technology Setup and Onboarding Fee
hiddenCosts.description: One-time fee for technology implementation and configuration paid to franchisor prior to opening.

hiddenCosts.name: Refresher / On-site Training Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Per-trainer daily fee plus travel/per diem for additional on-site training requested by franchisee.

hiddenCosts.name: Supplier Evaluation Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Non-refundable fee to evaluate an alternative supplier request, up to $1,500 per request not to exceed $2,500.

hiddenCosts.name: Non-Compliance Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $500 per compliance violation not cured within 30 days, then $250/week until cured.

hiddenCosts.name: Management Fee
hiddenCosts.description: 15%–25% of Gross Profit if franchisor must step in to operate the business due to franchisee default, death, or abandonment.

hiddenCosts.name: Environmental Response Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisor's actual costs plus 10% administrative fee if franchisor must respond to a spill, regulatory investigation, or safety violation caused by franchisee.

hiddenCosts.name: Renewal Fee
hiddenCosts.description: One-time fee payable upon execution of successor franchise agreement at end of each term.

hiddenCosts.name: Transfer Fee
hiddenCosts.description: 50% of then-current initial franchise fee plus broker, training, legal, and administrative costs upon transfer.

hiddenCosts.name: Annual Software Maintenance / Upgrade Costs
hiddenCosts.description: Estimated annual cost of required software maintenance, updating, and support contracts.

hiddenCosts.name: Pollution / Environmental Liability Insurance
hiddenCosts.description: Required pollution liability coverage with minimum $1,000,000 per occurrence; cost varies by market and volume.

hiddenCosts.name: SPCC Plan Consultant
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisee must engage a designated Spill Prevention, Control, and Countermeasure plan consultant if above-ground petroleum storage exceeds 1,320 gallons.

hiddenCosts.name: Opening Advertising (Initial 6 Months)
hiddenCosts.description: Minimum $750/month up to $10,000 aggregate during the opening advertising window, in addition to ongoing local marketing.

hiddenCosts.name: Breach of Territory Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Greater of $500 or 75% of amount paid by customer served outside territory without prior written permission.

operationalRisks.title: Brand-New Franchisor with No Franchised Units
operationalRisks.description: WetFuel Franchising, LLC was formed July 2025 and has zero franchised outlets; all Item 19 data comes from a single affiliate unit, making performance projections highly uncertain.

operationalRisks.title: Extensive Regulatory and Licensing Burden
operationalRisks.description: Franchisees must independently obtain motor fuel transporter licenses, environmental permits, fire marshal approvals, HazMat compliance, and state fuel tax registrations that vary significantly by state.

operationalRisks.title: Environmental and Spill Liability Exposure
operationalRisks.description: Mobile fuel delivery operations carry significant pollution, spill, and environmental liability risk requiring specialized insurance and SPCC plans; franchisor can charge back response costs plus 10%.

operationalRisks.title: Royalty on Gross Profit (Not Gross Sales) with Narrow COGS Definition
operationalRisks.description: Royalty is 9% of Gross Profit where COGS excludes labor, vehicle costs, insurance, and overhead — meaning the royalty base is broader than typical fuel-delivery margins suggest.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution in Idaho
operationalRisks.description: All disputes must be arbitrated or litigated in Boise, Idaho, which may disadvantage franchisees located in other states.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor Retains All Customer Data
operationalRisks.description: All customer data generated by the franchisee is owned exclusively by the franchisor, including post-termination, with real-time read access granted to franchisor at all times.

operationalRisks.title: Unilateral Operations Manual Changes
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor may amend the Operations Manual at any time; franchisee must implement changes within 30 days (90 days if capital expenditure exceeds $10,000 in 12 months).

operationalRisks.title: No Financing Offered; High Capital Requirement
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor provides no direct or indirect financing; total investment ranges $172,000–$490,500 with significant vehicle and equipment costs.

operationalRisks.title: Liquidated Damages on Termination
operationalRisks.description: If franchisor terminates for franchisee default, franchisee owes royalties and brand fund contributions for the lesser of 2 years or the remaining term.

operationalRisks.title: Territory Can Be Reduced for Underperformance
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor may reduce, modify, or reallocate territory if franchisee fails to meet minimum operational or performance standards.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor May Compete via National Accounts
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor reserves the right to service national/regional accounts and government contracts within franchisee's territory without compensation to franchisee.

operationalRisks.title: Broad Non-Compete (In-Term and Post-Term)
operationalRisks.description: In-term non-compete covers franchisee, all owners, and their spouses; post-term 2-year non-compete covers all WetFuel territories operating at termination date.

rentDetail.source: Item 7, p.17 (range $1,000–$3,000/month estimated for one month rent plus security deposit)

warnings: Item 21 discloses only an audited opening balance sheet dated 12/31/2025; no income statement or multi-year financials are available because the franchisor has been in business less than 3 years — auditor name and opinion could not be confirmed from the provided pages.
Item 19 is based on a single affiliate-owned outlet for approximately one year; no franchised outlet performance data exists.
Audited financial statements (Exhibit D) were referenced but not fully reproduced in the provided document pages, so balance sheet figures could not be extracted.
System has zero franchised units as of the FDD issuance date (March 10, 2026), making this a pre-launch franchise offering with no franchisee track record.

riskReasons: 4 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.
Franchisee unit economics could not be scored — no usable Item 19 (company/affiliate-owned only, or no FPR). Treat this as UNVERIFIED, not low-risk.
notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.
No Item 19 middle-cohort or network-average revenue found; economics are indeterminate.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $331,250 mid-point build-out, there is a $81,250 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal.

insights.conceptRationale: Mobile on-demand fuel delivery to commercial fleets and job sites is a B2B trade/field-service business with no retail storefront.

benchmark.label: Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Crew utilization, callback/warranty cost, and customer-acquisition cost. Low fixed overhead, but CAC and scheduling efficiency decide profitability.
benchmark.rampNote: 3–9 months to a steady job pipeline.
considerations: Budget materials/subcontractor COGS ~25–40% and field labor ~20–35%.
CAC (lead cost per booked job) is the dominant hidden line — ask Item 20 operators what they actually spend per job.
Confirm territory size, lead-generation expectations, and any required call-center/marketing fees.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–25% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

insights.staffingNote: Flagged as an lightly staffed model from the FDD, so labor is adjusted down to 10–21% — the 20–35% category band assumes a staffed venue. Confirm actual staffing with Item 20 franchisees.

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Read as lightly staffed from the FDD, which lowers the labor band applied below.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: We couldn't extract enough of this franchisor's financial statements to assess its condition. Request the audited Item 21 financials and have them reviewed before committing.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
